title: flexyBayes: Flexible Bayesian mixed models with ASReml and brms-style syntax
description: An R package for specifying mixed models in ASReml-style or brms-style formula syntax and estimating them via greta, INLA, or brms (Stan), with cross-engine posterior triangulation as the signature output. Variance components take a weakly-informative bounded-uniform prior on the SD scale by default, with penalised-complexity (Simpson et al. 2017), half-Cauchy, and other priors available. Supports Gaussian, binomial, Poisson, negative binomial, gamma, and beta families; structured covariance (us, fa, ar1); genomic relationship matrices (GBLUP); pedigree-based models; spatial models; and splines.

creators.name: Moldovan, Max
creators.affiliation: Australian Agricultural Genomics Institute (AAGI-AUS); Adelaide University

references: Simpson, D., Rue, H., Riebler, A., Martins, T. G., & Sørbye, S. H. (2017). Penalising model component complexity: a principled, practical approach to constructing priors. Statistical Science, 32(1), 1–28.

Rue, H., Martino, S., & Chopin, N. (2009). Approximate Bayesian inference for latent Gaussian models by using integrated nested Laplace approximations. Journal of the Royal Statistical Society: Series B, 71(2), 319–392.
notes: Development release for public package review. All exported user-facing APIs are experimental; see API_STABILITY.md and NEWS.md for scope and known limitations.
